page: Amazon Web Services r6in.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor, delivering 64 dedicated virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 54 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU score placing in the top 25%. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, and AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions, which contribute to strong performance in cryptographic operations, image processing, and ray tracing. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only large block bandwidth of 166.24 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 67.38 GB/sec. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 25% of tested systems.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1 and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, the r6in.16xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications. The lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must traverse the network, making it highly dependent on EBS optimization. It is well-suited for multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference, though single-core performance and raw memory bandwidth benchmarks fall within the average tier.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 32 physical cores operating at 3.5 GHz, supported by 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory. This configuration provides an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive applications. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU capabilities, database operations, and static web serving throughput, placing them in the top 25%. Conversely, single-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier. This server is optimized for workloads such as large-scale databases, in-memory caching, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for large databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform featuring high-speed networking and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network-heavy tasks
Strong multi-core CPU, database operations, and web serving benchmark performance
Designed for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications
